title: ageratina helenae
intro: Ageratina helenae, also known as Helen's woodflower or Tree Geranium, is a perennial plant native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). The medicinal properties of Ageratina helenae include its ability to reduce inflammation, act as an antioxidant, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, colds, and digestive issues, due to its numerous health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving respiratory function, and supporting immune system function. Ageratina helenae can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often used in traditional medicine, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and potential interactions.
